skill.name: when-gathering-requirements-use-interactive-planner
skill.description: Use Claude Code's AskUserQuestion tool to gather comprehensive requirements through structured multi-select questions.
skill.summary: Guide structured project discovery with interactive questions, validation steps, and final requirements documentation.
skill.author: DNYoussef

security_audit.summary: The static findings are false positives from Markdown formatting and ordinary descriptive text. The skill is documentation-only and contains no executable scripts, network calls, file operations, prompt injection text, or malicious intent.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Misidentified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged backticks are Markdown code fences around a process diagram and YAML metadata, not executable Ruby or shell code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding context is plain Markdown documentation. No command invocation syntax, interpreter directive, or execution path is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Inline Memory Keys Misidentified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged inline backticks list memory key names for requirements artifacts, not commands or dynamic execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The values are static documentation labels under a Memory Keys section. They are not passed to a shell or used by code.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Descriptive Text Misidentified as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged weak cryptography matches come from ordinary words such as description and descriptive, not cryptographic code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines contain human-readable requirement-gathering guidance. No hashing, encryption API, cipher configuration, or credential handling appears there.

content.value_statement: Unclear requirements cause scope gaps and rework. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through structured questions and validation.
actual_capabilities: Guides a five-phase requirements workflow from discovery through final documentation.
Prompts the assistant to ask clear initial and follow-up questions.
Organizes user responses into functional and non-functional requirements.
Adds validation steps for stakeholder review and approval.
Defines expected deliverables, including specifications and an action plan.
limitations: It is a documentation workflow, not an executable planning application.
It depends on user responses and cannot verify stakeholder approval independently.
It is designed around Claude Code's AskUserQuestion tool or an equivalent interaction method.
It does not estimate implementation effort from repository analysis.

use_cases.title: Clarify new product scope
use_cases.description: Collect goals, priorities, constraints, and acceptance criteria before creating a product specification.

use_cases.title: Refine feature requests
use_cases.description: Turn vague feature ideas into technical requirements, risks, milestones, and implementation questions.

use_cases.title: Prepare stakeholder reviews
use_cases.description: Structure discovery notes into a reviewable requirements document with open questions and validation checkpoints.

prompt_templates.title: Start Discovery
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the interactive planner to ask me initial questions about the project goal, users, scope, and success criteria.

prompt_templates.title: Clarify Technical Needs
prompt_templates.scenario: Move from product goals to implementation details.
prompt_templates.prompt: Ask follow-up questions about technical constraints, integrations, data needs, performance, security, and deployment expectations.

prompt_templates.title: Validate Requirements
prompt_templates.scenario: Check whether the collected requirements are complete.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the requirements gathered so far, identify gaps, and ask validation questions for stakeholders.

prompt_templates.title: Create Final Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Organize the confirmed requirements into a specification with acceptance criteria, risks, milestones, and an action plan.

output_examples.input: Gather requirements for a customer portal redesign.
output: Project goals and affected user groups identified.
Accessibility, performance, and integration constraints documented.

output_examples.input: Clarify a data export feature before engineering starts.
output: Export formats, filters, permissions, and data volume assumptions listed.
Open technical risks and compliance questions highlighted.
Acceptance criteria and next implementation steps drafted.

output_examples.input: Turn a vague automation idea into a project brief.
output: Business objective and target workflow documented.
Inputs, outputs, dependencies, and constraints captured.

best_practices: Start with project goals and affected users before discussing implementation details.
Use multi-select questions when several priorities or constraints may apply.
Validate the final requirements with stakeholders before creating the action plan.
anti_patterns: Skipping discovery and asking only technical questions.
Treating unanswered assumptions as approved requirements.
Creating milestones before acceptance criteria and constraints are clear.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create requirements specifications, technical notes, validation questions, and action plans.

faq.question: Does it run commands or inspect my repository?
faq.answer: No. The skill is a documentation workflow and does not include executable code.

faq.question: Can it work outside Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes, if the assistant can ask structured questions. Claude Code support is the primary documented workflow.

faq.question: How long should a session take?
faq.answer: The documented workflow estimates 15 to 30 minutes for discovery, clarification, validation, and documentation.

faq.question: What information should I provide first?
faq.answer: Start with the project goal, target users, scope boundaries, timeline, and any known constraints.

faq.question: Does it replace stakeholder review?
faq.answer: No. It includes stakeholder validation as a required step before final planning.
